title: retama angulata
intro: Retama angulata, also known as white broom or Jacob's spurge, is a flowering plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions like acne and eczema. In herbal preparations, Retama angulata is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be taken internally or applied topically to reap its medicinal benefits.
